text:
I have a plan.

Submit a TWA for 1 ada. Copy and paste the previously approved NCL of 350 million ADA.

Why a TWA?

The TWA becomes valid at an epoch boundary, where an info action must run for 6 epoch.

If we submit it now and rally the entire ecosystem, we can get it approved within an epoch.

I am happy to submit it, but I do not have the ADA for deposit. If someone else woukd submit, or if I had donors (I woukd return your ada after), I would happily submit this.

The constitution only requires the NCL be a governance action, not an info action. A treasury withdrawal is a governance action, and thus the fastest way to approve a new NCL since a treasury withdrawal becomes valid as soon as it hits the threshold.

text:
Net Change Limit Status

The ECC team recently discussed the status of Cardano's Net Change Limit (NCL) and have determined that there is currently NO active NCL. This means that we will be required to find the treasury withdrawal governance actions currently on-chain as unconstitutional.

The previously active NCL (gov_action169kllwhfmp488je5x5rwvufd08p8sztdcf0ghf5sp6ey2gnjdwaqql47xry) ended at the conclusion of epoch 612. While 2 governance actions were recently submitted to establish a new NCL, neither have met the criteria necessary to be considered ratified.

While the Info action titled "Net Change Limit (Epoch 613 to Epoch 713)" (gov_action1m3xx08yv788vfxqh6nfvrjtvmqpwezsy0ggaczctkyjmttc2wmxsq4jsr7q) did meet the 50% DRep approval requirement, it did NOT meet the constitutionality assessment threshold by the Constitutional Committee.

The latest NCL governance action titled "Net Change Limit of 300 Million ADA for Epochs 613–713" (gov_action1wwjwkg2g0qwr0mehey9r8gwn6qz3r28wl6wdl2s75kfmpy8j87tqqtft9tp) did NOT meet the 50% DRep approval requirement.

author: riskgamingco

text:
@astroboysoup The proposal explicitly states that "Draper Dragon may also co-invest capital from its existing private funds alongside the Orion Fund deals." This is a material structural risk that deserves a clear public answer before any Treasury withdrawal is approved.

The concern is not co-investment itself. It is the absence of any disclosed policy governing how conflicts are managed when the same GP controls both the public Treasury vehicle and a parallel private vehicle investing in the same portfolio company.

Three questions need answers before Tranche 1 is approved. First, will the LPA require the GP to disclose any intended co-investment from affiliated private funds before a deal closes in the Orion Fund, not after? Without pre-deal disclosure, the community has no ability to assess whether the terms offered to the Orion Fund are equivalent to those offered to the private vehicle. Second, will the LPA include a cap on the combined equity position that Draper Dragon entities, across all vehicles public and private, can hold in any single portfolio company? Without this, the GP can accumulate an outsized position through multiple small entries, each individually below any disclosure threshold, while the Treasury's exposure remains fixed at the initial ticket. Third, standard institutional LPAs include an LP Advisory Committee with authority to review and approve conflicted transactions. Given that Arouet Holdings is effectively the sole LP representing $75M in public capital, what mechanism exists for independent review of deals where GP co-investment creates a conflict? The Community Director on the Arouet Holdings board has visibility but no explicit authority over investment decisions, and this gap needs to be addressed in the LPA before any funds are deployed.

These are not hypothetical risks. The co-investment right described in the proposal is the primary mechanism through which a GP can extract value from a public ecosystem fund beyond the disclosed 20% carried interest. If the LPA does not address this explicitly, each portfolio founder will face these conflicts alone at the term sheet stage, with no recourse to fund-level protections.

I think before Tranche 1 (if approved), publish the draft LPA or provide written commitments on these three points. The side letter with Arouet Holdings being public is welcome, but the side letter governs Treasury repayment mechanics, not the investment conduct of the GP. The LPA governs conduct. That is the document that needs transparency now.

author: InputEndorsers

text:
🗳️ Attention Cardno DReps: New Governance Action

The Orion Fund proposal (led by Draper Dragon) is now on-chain. Key takeaways for voters:

✅ Tranche-based: Approving ONLY 50M ADA ($15M) for now. Future funds require new votes. 
✅ Repayment: Treasury is repaid 100% of capital before the GP takes profit. 
✅ Governance: Includes a Community-appointed director for Arouet Holdings. 
✅ Transparency: Dashboard, reporting and metrics every step of the way to show progress.
✅ Capped: Total multi-year exposure capped at 175M ADA.

This conversation delves into the $80 million fund launched by Draper Dragon and the Cardano Foundation to boost ecosystem projects on the Cardano blockchain. The discussion covers the fund's structure, investment strategies, governance mechanisms, and the importance of community engagement. Key insights include a breakdown of investments by category, the role of equity investments in startup acceleration, and the establishment of a transparent public dashboard for accountability. The conversation emphasises the need for professional management in venture funding to ensure sustainable growth and support for Cardano projects.  

Takeaways 
✅ The $80 million fund aims to enhance Cardano's ecosystem. 
✅ Draper Dragon has a strong background in digital asset investments. 
✅ The fund will operate in tranches to manage capital deployment effectively. 
✅ Direct investments will focus on Cardano native companies. 
✅ Equity investments will be made in startup acceleration programs. 
✅ A public dashboard will track fund performance and ecosystem impact. 
✅ Governance will involve community oversight through a special purpose vehicle. 
✅ The fund aims to return capital to the treasury and support ecosystem growth. 
✅ Community feedback has shaped the proposal's final structure. 
✅ Professional management is crucial for successful venture funding.  

Chapters 
00:00 Introduction to the $80 Million Fund 
05:52 Understanding Draper Dragon's Role 
11:18 Investment Structure and Tranche Breakdown 
15:40 Equity Investments and Startup Acceleration 
20:56 Governance and Accountability Mechanisms 
25:07 Performance Metrics and Community Protections 
30:09 Draper University and Ecosystem Development 
35:23 Final Thoughts and Community Support

Deep dive into the metadata here: 🔗 https://t.co/uSRGfngWrm

text:
A good example of how crypto investment articles should be structured:

• Clear comparison of projects
• Real metrics (capital flows, network usage, adoption)
• Discussion of risks and uncertainties
• Full disclosures about holdings or conflicts of interest
• Clear distinction between analysis and opinion

This isn’t an endorsement of the outlet or the conclusion, just an example of proper formatting and transparency.

You may agree or disagree with the take, but responsible financial writing should include research, context, and disclosure.

Markets mature when information is presented responsibly — not through clickbait headlines or recycled hype narratives.

text:
Mezzamine is Essential for Miners ⚒️

Borrowing USD to fund a BTC business is a structural trap. 📉

Miners have historically earned in BTC while owing in USD. When Bitcoin drops, revenue falls—but dollar debt stays fixed. The 2022 “death spiral” proved how deadly this mismatch can be.

Mezzamine fixes it:

Borrow in BTC, Repay in BTC ⛓️: Eliminate the currency mismatch.

Operational Stability 🛡️: No more dumping BTC at the bottom to cover a dollar payment.

author: ShugaAyomi

text:
🚨🌐Update report 101, March 6, 2026：Cardanoの次のハードフォーク準備と2026年予算プロセスの進展

Intersectの週次レポート（Update #101）が公開され、Cardanoエコシステムの技術・ガバナンス・開発の進捗がまとめられています。少し紹介が遅れましたが、今回のポイントを整理すると、いくつか重要な動きが見えてきます。

まず技術面では、Cardano Node 10.7.0のリリースが近づいています。

これは次のハードフォーク（通称 Van Rossemアップグレード / Protocol Version 11）の基盤となる重要なバージョンで、Preview → PreProd → Mainnetの順に適用される予定です。

10.7.0は単なるハードフォーク対応ではなく、新機能も含む大きなノードアップデートで、今後のエコシステム拡張の土台になる位置づけです。リリース後はウォレットやツールなど各プロジェクトが統合テストを進め、ネットワーク全体の準備状況を確認していく流れになります。

また、ハードフォーク準備を進めるHard Fork Working Groupは今後毎週ミーティングを行い、さらにコミュニティ向けに毎週Xスペースも開催するとのことです。

アップグレードの透明性を高めるための取り組みと言えそうです。
ガバナンス面では、憲法改正プロセス（CAP）の整備が進んでいます。

改正提案を提出する際に必要な情報や、変更内容の規模に応じたコミュニティ相談期間などが定義され、Cardano憲法を改正するための正式な手順が整備されつつあります。

また、憲法委員会選挙の候補者登録項目も承認されました。
候補者の背景や憲法理解度などを公開することで、有権者が判断しやすくなる仕組みです。

現在進行中の主なガバナンスアクションは次の3つです。

① Net Change Limit（NCL）

2027年までのトレジャリー引き出し総額を3億ADAに制限する「ガードレール」を設定する提案。

② Amaruノード開発資金（約1014万ADA）

Rustベースの新しいCardanoノード「Amaru」を開発するための資金提案。
ノード多様性やネットワークの堅牢性向上が目的です。

③ Cardano予算プロセスの枠組み（Info Action）

2026年以降のエコシステム予算をどう管理するかという全体設計の提案。
特に注目されるのが、この新しい予算プロセスです。

Intersectは、従来の「個別プロジェクトが助成金を取りに行くモデル」から、
・標準化された提案フォーマット
・コミュニティレビュー
・DRepによる優先順位付け
・NCLとの整合確認
という段階的なプロセスを導入し、
2030ビジョンに沿った戦略的投資に移行しようとしています。

2026年サイクルでは、
4月：提案募集
↓
コミュニティレビュー
↓
DRep投票（Ekklesia）
↓
6月：トレジャリー引き出し提案
という流れが想定されています。

開発面では、2025年トレジャリー資金で進められていたプロジェクトの1つ、Maestro Web3 Developer Stackが完了しました。

このプロジェクトでは
・ブロックチェーンインデクサ
・メンプール監視
・イベント通知システム
・ウォレット管理ツール
などが提供され、dApp開発のインフラを大きく簡素化するツール群が公開されています。

特にBitcoin対応インデクサが含まれている点は興味深いところです。

エコシステム面では、PythオラクルのCardano統合も進んでいます。

Pythは数千のリアルタイム価格データをスマートコントラクトに提供するTier1オラクルで、統合が進めばDeFiアプリの機能拡張に大きく貢献しそうです。

この統合をテーマに、3月にはアルゼンチンでハッカソン「Pythathon」も予定されています。

コミュニティ面では、Intersect委員会選挙（2026）の準備も進んでいます。

3月30日から候補者応募が開始される予定で、
・応募時の役割確認
・参加履歴の可視化
・多言語申請フォーム
などの改善が導入されるとのことです。

そして日本コミュニティに関係する話として、
2月25日に銀座でEMURGO主導のDRep予算プロセスイベントも開催されました。

ここでは、2025年予算サイクルで問題となった「DRep疲れ」などを踏まえ、

・2030ビジョンに基づく予算配分
・体系化された予算プロセス
・戦略投資としてのトレジャリー利用

という方向への転換が議論されています。

今回のレポートを見ると、
・Protocol Version 11（Van Rossem）に向けた技術準備
・憲法改正プロセス整備
・2026予算制度の設計
・開発インフラ整備
・オラクルなど外部統合
と、Cardanoは技術・ガバナンス・エコシステムの3つを同時に前進させている段階に入っていることがよく分かります。

今後数ヶ月は、ハードフォーク準備と2026予算プロセスの動きが、エコシステム全体の重要なテーマになりそうです。
author: SIPO_Tokyo

text:
🚨🌐Cardanoアップグレード最新状況：SanchoNetでProtocol Version 11テスト開始

Cardanoの次期アップグレードに向けた進捗が公開されました。

今回のアップデートで一番のポイントは、ハードフォーク候補（Hard Fork Candidate）のプレリリースがまもなく登場するという点です。

現在、テストネット SanchoNet は最新のCardanoノードにアップグレードされており、Protocol Version 11 の機能テストがすでに始まっています。

また、Plutusの新しいコストモデルも展開準備が進んでおり、SanchoNetではすでに利用可能に��っています。
ノードの状況を整理すると次のようになります。

まず Cardano Node 10.6.2。

こちらはすでに約4週間前にリリースされています。
ハードフォーク関連の機能を含んでいますが、メインネット用の最終候補ではありません。現在はSanchoNetなどで継続的なテストが行われている段階です。
そして次に重要なのが Cardano Node 10.7.0。

約1週間以内のリリースが予定されており、今回のハードフォーク準備の中心になるバージョンと見られています。

安定したプレリリースが公開されると、まず
Preview
↓
PreProd
↓
Mainnet
という順番でネットワークがアップグレードされる予定です。

また、Node 10.7.0に対応する DBSyncの新バージョンも続いてリリースされる予定で、ウォレットやインフラ、DAppなどのエコシステムツールの統合ポイントになる見込みです。

Intersectは今回の10.7.0について、単なる修正リリースではなく、ハードフォーク機能に加えて新しい機能も含む大型アップデートになると説明しています。

そのためノードが公開された後は、エコシステム全体で
・ツール統合
・互換性テスト
・パフォーマンス検証
などを進めていくことが重要になります。

ハードフォークワーキンググループは現在も毎週ミーティングを継続しており、情報共有のために定期的なXスペースも開催されています。

Cardanoのアップグレードは、
単にコードを更新するだけではなく、
SPO、開発者、インフラ、ツールなどエコシステム全体が連携して進めるプロセス
でもあります。

Node 10.7のリリースは、次のハードフォークに向けた実質的なスタートラインになりそうですね。

text:
@silversoul8668 It's a more nuanced discussion for sure.

Neither my take is right as well as thinking a reset is the silver bullet that solves all problems.

If we reset voting power and don't change any of the underlying logic or add an incentive scheme we could end up in an even worse situation than the status quo!

I want more voting power allocated more evenly in the ecosystem, but we should not create an environment where we end up with even more control in the hand of a few DReps.

If you or others don't believe me just look at the CF delegation program to DReps. We are delegating more than 300 million ada to community DReps

author: StakeWithPride

text:
@Hardfork_OT One of the main things I took away from this is "the filters are too weak".

This is something that I know we still struggle with in building the process.  Intersect is ensuring that the framework will follow standards that fit the Vision and Mission of Cardano, which was approved by DReps, but we still have a lot of other things to consider.

I have to be honest and say that this year is still a learning year.  Last year was too broad, based on the feedback we recieved from DReps and Ada Holders.  You are right that we must filter for quality.  But at the same time, we must not allow the filter to keep good ideas from being considered and show too much favoritism to established vendors.

Next year, we will have more data to build more filters.  Some will be filters that reject proposals that are not good enough quality.  Some will be filters that push proposers to seek funding from other sources (i.e. Catalyst or CB DAO).  Others may be filters to just put proposals in the right categories for consideration.

I know our process may be slow and imperfect but I'd like to build to be better and better rather than try to solve all the problems at once and not know why new problems have come up!

text:
This is very important news and should not go unnoticed.

Until now, when speaking with developers or companies about RWA, the preferred development network was often Polygon. This was not necessarily due to superior technical capabilities, but rather because of the ease provided by having a tokenization standard such as ERC-3643. Today, those limitations are being addressed.

This development introduces a framework that, over time, can become a preferred standard and significantly simplify the commercial and technical decision to build on Cardano.

Welcome, CIP-0113.

Well done @Cardano_CF 🏆

text:
@JasonrShuman You could prob achieve what you're trying to do here with behaviour-based interview questions. You don't want questions that have a 'right' answer. You want questions that reveal how they think. In addition, asking about the past rather than future behaviour is a more reliable axis. 

Examples
- "Describe a time when you had to resolve a difficult problem on your own."
- "Tell me about a time when you felt you were unable to meet your responsibilities."

80% of this is the people doing the interviewing knowing what they want and not being lazy. Another 10% is a consistent process.

author: Cardano_Power

text:
Building an alternative Cardano node is a significant challenge. 

The IOG team used formal methods, academic research, and peer review when designing and building the Haskell node. 

Today, virtually all block-producing nodes (100%) in the Cardano network run the Haskell implementation.

Therefore, it is almost impossible not to approve the budget for IOG.

Alternative implementations such as Amaru, Dingo, Gerolamo, Acropolis, and others must follow the same ledger rules, consensus rules, and networking protocols so that they remain fully compatible with the existing network. 

Put simply, alternative nodes must do exactly what the Haskell node does, according to the specification.

Deviations from the expected behavior can create risks, including consensus inconsistencies or network instability.

Node diversity can make the network more robust, but only if alternative implementations are well designed, thoroughly tested, and carefully maintained. 

Adding a new node does not automatically mean higher robustness.

This places high demands on development teams in terms of engineering quality, testing, and long-term maintenance.

Thus, introducing a new implementation into the network inevitably introduces some level of risk. Always!

A prudent approach is gradual adoption. For example, the network could initially consist of something like:

▪️90% Haskell node
▪️10% alternative node

If an inconsistency or implementation bug occurs, the majority of nodes would still behave consistently, reducing the risk of widespread disruption.

It would also be necessary to monitor the network for an extended period of time. Only after sufficient testing and operational experience would it be reasonable to encourage more SPOs to adopt the alternative implementation and increase its stake share.

It is, of course, possible to introduce multiple alternative nodes at the same time, but doing so may increase the complexity and risk during the early stages of deployment.

Higher node diversity has both positive and negative aspects.

Higher node diversity can potentially negatively impact the speed of protocol evolution. Cardano needs to have higher network throughput and faster transaction finality. 

So, alternative nodes might slow us down.

Each new protocol feature/version must be implemented correctly in all node implementations used by the network. 

For example, if a future version of Ouroboros Leios were introduced, the consensus changes would need to be implemented consistently across all active node implementations.

If one development team falls significantly behind, it could delay the deployment of the new feature across the network. 

For this reason, it is important that teams developing alternative nodes have sufficient long-term funding and engineering capacity.

Once the DReps decide to support additional node implementations, maintaining them becomes an ongoing responsibility. 

Delivering new protocol features will require not only funding the core implementation (Haskell node) but also ensuring that other supported node teams can implement and maintain the same features. 

I want to be clear. In the long run, striving for greater node diversity is a reasonable goal.

If SPOs are willing to run different node implementations, the network could eventually reach a healthier distribution such as:

▪️33% Haskell node
▪️33% alternative node A
▪️33% alternative node B

Or even better:

▪️25% Haskell node
▪️25% alternative node A
▪️25% alternative node B
▪️25% alternative node C

In my view, supporting an alternative node is not about submitting a Treasury Withdrawal. By doing so, we are essentially establishing an alternative team to IOG in terms of costs.

Treasury Withdrawal is the allocation of funds without strategic thinking, collective consensus, prioritization, and planning.

Fund allocation should be the last step, not the first, when it comes to such a serious decision.

DReps decide on fund allocation without any prior steps. This is not ideal for most proposals. In the case of decisions on alternative nodes, this is an irresponsible approach.

author: adamKDean

text:
@Cardano_Jitsu I think this raises a very important issue.

In Cardano governance, DRep discovery — the ability for users to easily find and understand DReps — is still not sufficiently developed.

In that sense, the idea of wallets providing a “DRep menu” that displays information such as DRep descriptions, voting history, and governance stance makes a lot of sense.

In fact, Daedalus (which has many large Japanese ADA holders using it) is already considering UI improvements in this direction.

Allowing users to understand DReps before choosing to delegate to them is extremely important for maintaining healthy governance.

CGOV Monitoring Layer v2.0
https://t.co/bhfOZhLzg2

At the same time, there is another layer to this issue.

That is the DRep incentive structure.

If the system naturally favors large DReps, then even if the UI presents a diverse set of DReps, delegation may ultimately still concentrate over time.

For this reason, I am also proposing mechanisms aimed at maintaining governance health from perspectives such as:

• DRep reward design
• Delegation concentration monitoring

DRep Reward Design Proposal V2
https://t.co/NuRtcsJAsH

In my view, we need both:
Wallet-level DRep discovery
Protocol-level incentive design

When these two elements work together, we can move toward a more decentralized and resilient governance system.

I hope this discussion continues to grow within the community.

とても重要な問題提起だと思います。

Cardanoガバナンスにおいて、
DRep discovery（DRepを見つけられる仕組み）は確かにまだ十分ではありません。

その意味で、ウォレットが
DRepの説明、投票履歴、スタンスなどを表示する「DRepメニュー」を提供するというアイデアは、とても理にかなっていると思います。

実際、Daedalus（日本のクジラユーザーが多い）ではこの方向のUI改善が検討されています。

ユーザーがDRepを理解した上で選択できるようにすることは、ガバナンスの健全性にとって非常に重要です。

CGOV Monitoring Layer v2.0
https://t.co/bhfOZhLzg2

一方で、この問題にはもう一つのレイヤーもあります。

それは
DRepインセンティブ構造の問題です。

もし大規模DRepが自然に優位になる構造のままだと、たとえUIで多様なDRepを表示しても、最終的には集中が進む可能性があります。

そのため、私は現在
• DRep報酬設計
• 集中度モニタリング

などの観点から、ガバナンスの健全性を保つ仕組みについても提案しています。

DRep Reward Design Proposal V 2
https://t.co/NuRtcsJAsH

ウォレットの DRep discovery と
プロトコルレベルの インセンティブ設計

この両方が揃うことで、より分散化されたガバナンスが実現できると思います。

この議論がコミュニティで広がることを期待しています。

text:
@mattpiz @rvcas @KylixAfonso The Aiken implementation is objectively less efficient across the board.  

In the nominal case transaction, which spends a single programmable tokens UTxO with a single programmable token policy in it, the Plutarch implementation exhibits:  

11.14% reduction in ex-mem and 14.88% reduction in CPU against the optimized Aiken variant.   

Benchmarks for non-nominal cases (ie. transfer of 25 UTxOs with programmable tokens) benchmark at:
42.18% rection in ex-mem.   

ThirdPartyTransfer of 64 UTxOs benchmark at >82% reduction in both ex-cpu and ex-mem compared to the Aiken contracts, because the Aiken implementation is O(n * m) where n is the total number of inputs and m is the number of inputs from programmable logic base (programmable token UTxOs), and the Plutarch implementation is O(m). 

The information can be verified here: 

Nominal case benchmarks for aiken:
Tidy and optimise (mem: -25.75%, cpu: -21.01%) (#20) · cardano-foundation/cip113-programmable-tokens@e74dc3e  

Nominal case benchmarks & various multi-UTxO benchmarks for Plutarch: 
Benchmarks, tests and mint support for ProgrammableLogicBase by colll78 · Pull Request #107 · input-output-hk/wsc-poc

text:
@NaVi_GaT0R Hi.  We actually agree here.  

The Founding Entities actually want to do less over time and have the strong community builders like yourself take on more active roles and prominence within the Cardano ecosystem.

We have had the exact discussions within the Pentad that there are certain integration work where we would welcome community builders to take on (and be remunerated).   This is part of Pentad 2.0.   We are listening. 

Having said that, there remain contracts where the Founding Entities or Intersect remain the counter party of choice for vendors outside of our community.  This is because the size, duration and liabilities would be far too great to ask any single project or even a collection of projects to take on.  Unfortunately, institutional / enterprise vendors do not see DAOs as reliable legal counter parts.  This is also especially important where the interests of Cardano may supersede specific needs of individual projects.  The truth is that is it impossible to satisfy everyone.

I will also speak about large integrations where the quantums are in the 7 and 8 figures with multi-year commitments.  For contracts of this size and complexity that involve the entirety of the Cardano ecosystem; for better or worse; the #Pentad is the only collection of entities that possess the legal, technical, and commercial infrastructure in place to act on behalf of the community.  Respectfully,  @IOHK_Charles and @F_Gregaard are lions who advocate tirelessly for Cardano and I will stand with them every day of the week.  

As to investment, there is a proposal from @DraperDragon where investment will be professionally made into our ecosystem with real support from subject matter experts.  This is a big step up from Catalyst and I think that if they enter Cardano as venture studio, we would be stronger. 

There is more to come where we will be reaching out to the top builders in Cardano to come together and showcase what you are building on the global stage. 

#strongertogether

text:
1. We wanted a chain that could keep its footing.

If you’re building something meant to last, reliability is not optional. It’s the ground under your boots.

Cardano mainnet has been producing blocks since September 2017. And during the November 2025 network incident, block production continued, the network never fully went offline, and convergence was restored in roughly 14 hours, with no reported loss of funds.

That matters to us.

Because we’re not building for a weekend spike. We’re building for persistence. For continuity. For a world people can return to again and again without wondering whether the floorboards are going to give way under them.

We wanted infrastructure with a pulse, not a paper costume.

text:
🚨🌐Cardanoのステーブル流動性が拡大：USDCx–USDMプールTVLが500万ドル突破

SundaeSwapが公開したデータによると、Cardano上の USDCx–USDMステーブルプールのTVLが500万ドルを超えた とのことです。

このプールは「StablePool」と呼ばれる仕組みで、通常のAMMとは少し設計が違います。

価格がほぼ同じ資産（ステーブルコインなど）を対象にしているため、以下のような特徴があります。

・スリッページが小さい
・資本効率が高い
・価格が安定しやすい

つまり、USDCxとUSDMのように価格がほぼ1ドルに連動する資産同士の交換に最適化された流動性プールです。

今回のポイントは、単にTVLが増えたというよりも、Cardanoのステーブルコイン基盤が徐々に形になってきているという点です。

Cardanoではここ最近、
・USDM（Mehenのステーブルコイン）
・USDCx（ブリッジ型USDC）
など、複数のステーブル資産が並び始めています。

そしてその「交換インフラ」として、SundaeSwapのようなDEXのStablePoolが機能し始めると、
・DeFiの決済基盤
・レンディング
・トレード
・流動性提供
といったエコシステムの土台が整いやすくなります。

特にステーブル同士の深い流動性は、
DeFi全体の摩擦を減らす重要なインフラです。

USDCx × USDM のようなペアは、今後
・レンディングプロトコル
・決済
・クロスチェーン流動性
などとも結びつきやすい領域です。

TVL500万ドルはまだ巨大とは言えませんが、Cardano DeFiの視点では「ステーブル流動性が積み上がり始めた」サインとして見ておく価値がある動きだと思います。

text:
Cardano Daily - 2026-03-10

Cardano 正处于关键转折点，围绕国库分配、资金机制和生态可持续性的治理辩论日益激烈。社区正在努力平衡财政保守主义与战略投资需求，同时新的治理工具正在涌现以提高透明度和参与度。

🏗️ 生态发展
生态系统正在经历资金寒冬,Project Catalyst 暂停至第三季度等待新预算流程。随着开发者寻求可持续的薪酬模式,人才流失问题日益严重。多个替代节点实现方案被提出以增强网络韧性,包括 HLabs 的 TypeScript 节点 Gerolamo 和 Blink Labs 的 Golang 节点 Dingo。CIP-0113 可编程代币标准已发布,支持符合监管的代币化,但实现方式引发争议。Midnight 和比特币 DeFi 集成仍是扩展 Cardano 影响力的战略重点。

⚖️ 治理讨论
Intersect 提交了更新的 2026 年预算框架,引入标准化提案要求、KPI 和基于里程碑的国库资金。Yoroi 钱包 DRep 投票支持该框架文档化,而关于适当的净变化限额(NCL)的辩论仍在继续,提案范围从 3 亿到 4 亿 ADA。Nomos 治理平台上线,提供统一界面追踪提案、投票和 DRep 活动,无需钱包。关于提案是否应遵循 Intersect 的中心化流程还是保持无需许可的国库访问,紧张局势持续存在。社区呼吁提高风险承受能力,战略性投资营销、开发者关系和产品采用,而非无休止的基础设施建设。

💬 社区声音
社区成员正在辩论基础设施投资与面向用户的产品开发之间的平衡。越来越多的呼声要求国库资金支持永续合约、预测市场和代币发射台等能推动实际交易量和采用的产品。Iagon-Fireblocks 集成争议凸显了资金决策的紧张局势,尽管 Intersect 建议批准,Pentad 仍拒绝报销。DRep 们在没有明确协调的情况下难以评估竞争性节点实现提案。关于流动性质押对治理参与和投票权集中影响的担忧持续浮现。

🌐 行业观察
Cardano 是迈阿密海滩 FutureProof 会议上唯一代表的 L1 区块链,显示出企业采用潜力。EMURGO 在东京主导了关键预算讨论,规划 2026 年 DRep 角色和资本分配。生态系统在更广泛的 Web3 格局中日益被视为孤岛,观察者在观望但犹豫进入。社区成员强调网络必须作为具有销售和业务开发职能的企业运营,而非仅仅是工程项目。宪法委员会正在考虑将最小规模从 7 人减少到 6 人,以提高对临时空缺的韧性。

text:
🚨🌐🌒チャールズ・ホスキンソン氏動画「Welcome Binance」解説・全翻訳BinanceがMidnightを歓迎：Cardanoネイティブ資産「NIGHT」Binance上場へ──エコシステムにとって歴史的マイルストーン

チャールズ・ホスキンソン氏の最新動画が公開されました。内容はかなり大きなニュースです。

Cardanoのプライバシーサイドチェーン Midnight のトークン NIGHT が、
世界最大の取引所 Binanceのスポット市場に上場予定 と発表されました。

しかもこれは、
Binanceに上場する最初のCardanoネイティブアセットになります。

まず整理しておくと、
・Midnightローンチ
・NIGHTがBinance Alphaに掲載
・コミュニティでは「いつBinanceスポットに来るのか」が議論
・そして今回、Binanceが正式発表
という流れです。

BNBホルダー向けの
Midnight Holder Airdropも同時に発表されました。

Binanceは言うまでもなく、暗号資産市場の中心的な存在です。

ホスキンソン氏によると
・アルトコイン取引の約80%がBinance
・ユーザー数 約5億人
という巨大市場です。

つまり今回の出来事は、
Cardanoネイティブ資産が
世界最大の流動性市場に入る
という意味を持ちます。

ホスキンソンはこの出来事を、
・2021年のADAのCoinbase上場
・最近のUSDCxの動き
と並ぶ、エコシステムの重要な節目だと語っています。

そしてもう一つ重要なのが、
Midnightが単なるトークンプロジェクトではない点です。

Midnightのテーマは
・プライバシー
・ゼロ知識証明（ZK）
・コンプライアンス

つまり
スマートコンプライアンス
です。

「情報を公開せずに証明する」
「規制にも対応する」
という仕組みで、
ホスキンソン氏は
10兆ドル規模のリアルワールド資産が
この分野に入る可能性がある
と語っています。

さらに動画後半では、
Midnightの面白い構想も語られています。
それが Midnight City。

これは
エージェント文明（Agentic Civilization）
と呼ばれる世界です。

ユーザーは
・NIGHTでAIエージェントを購入
・都市に配置
・毎月チャージ
という形で参加します。

ホスキンソンはこれを
「たまごっちみたいなもの」
と説明しています。

つまり
AIエージェントが活動するデジタル都市
というコンセプトです。

そして動画の中でホスキンソンは、
Cardanoへの批判にも触れました。

これまで多くの人が
「Cardanoは10億ドル規模のプロジェクトを作れない」
「Tier1流動性は取れない」
「DeFiは終わった」
と言ってきた。

しかし今回の出来事について、
彼はこう言っています。
「今日、それが間違いだったことを示した」

今回のポイントはシンプルです。
Cardanoネイティブアセットが
ついにBinance市場に入る。
これはエコシステムにとって
かなり大きなマイルストーンです。

そしてもう一つ大きいのは、
最初の一例ができたこと。

つまり今後、
他のCardanoネイティブアセットが
Binanceに上場する可能性も現実的になってきます。

Cardanoの歴史の中でも
なかなか面白い瞬間だと思います。

動画の翻訳と解説を記事にまとめましたので、
ぜひ読んでみてください。
👇
https://t.co/yAoWjXYntA

text:
🚨💡🌐DeFiは“機能”ではなく“種”から始まる──IOGが示す次世代DeFiの設計思想

IOGのDeFiプロダクトコンサルタント Rusty氏が、かなり興味深い技術ブログを公開しました。

テーマは「DeFiは“作るもの”ではなく、“植えるもの”である」という考え方です。

現在のDeFiは、
・マージンレンディング
・AMM（自動マーケットメーカー）
・パーペチュアル先物
といったTradFiの機能を個別にコピーして積み上げる構造になっています。

Rusty氏は、これを
「木をクローンで作ろうとしている状態」
だと表現しています。

見た目はそれらしくても、
・壊れやすい
・投機中心になる
・持続的な経済にならない
という問題が生まれるという指摘です。

そこで提案されているのが
DeFi Kernel（DeFiのコア基盤）という発想です。

Rusty氏は、経済が成長するために必要な最小要素は
たった3つのプリミティブ（基本要素）だと説明しています。

1：Payments（決済）
2：Credit Market（信用市場）
3：Asset Exchange（資産交換市場）

ブロックチェーンはすでに決済機能（Payments）を持っています。

つまりDeFiが本当に必要なのは
・信用市場
・資産取引市場
この2つを正しく設計することだという考えです。

ここでRusty氏が強く主張しているのが
「どんな信用市場でも良いわけではない」という点です。

現在のDeFiのレンディングの多くは
マージン型（価格変動で即清算される）です。

しかしこれは
・トレードには向く
・事業には向かない
という問題があります。

例えば
事業資金を借りたのに
担保価格が一時的に下がっただけで
自動清算される

これでは
企業活動のための信用市場にはなりません。

そこでDeFi Kernelでは
Non-margin credit market（非マージン型信用市場）
を採用します。

つまり
・固定条件のローン
・価格ではなく返済不履行で清算
という
伝統的な事業ローンに近い構造です。

Rusty氏はここで興味深い比較を出しています。

TradFi
民間ローンの約95%が非マージン

DeFi
約95%がマージンローン

つまり現在のDeFiは
構造的に投機しか生まれない設計になっているという指摘です。

もう一つの重要なポイントが
取引所の設計です。

現在のDeFiは
AMM（流動性プール）が主流ですが
AMMには
本質的なスリッページ
があります。

例えば
100ドルの取引で
1%スリッページを抑えるには
約1万ドルの流動性が必要
という構造です。

そして問題は
信用市場と組み合わさると
これが負のループを生むことです。

価格下落
↓
マージン清算
↓
AMM売り
↓
スリッページ
↓
さらに価格下落
↓
追加清算

つまり
ボラティリティを増幅する構造
が生まれます。

そこでDeFi Kernelは
Order Book（板取引）
を採用します。

板取引なら
・価格指定注文
・スリッページ無し
・大口清算でも市場破壊しにくい
というメリットがあります。

さらにRusty氏は
DeFiの土壌（ブロックチェーン設計）についても言及しています。

重要なのは
・ローカルステート
・スマートコントラクト
・意図的に遅いL1
です。

特に面白いのが
「速すぎるブロックチェーンは信用市場に向かない」
という指摘です。

理由は
信用市場は
・数ヶ月
・数年
という期間
履歴を記憶する必要があるからです。

しかし高速チェーンは
・データ量が膨大
・古い状態を削除（pruning）
してしまう。

つまり
信用履歴が消えてしまう可能性がある
という問題です。

そのためRusty氏は
L1は
遅くても強固で記憶力のある基盤
であるべきだと説明しています。

そして
高速処理は
Layer2で行う
という構造です。

これは
・Visa / Mastercard
・FedWire
の関係に似ています。

Rusty氏の結論はシンプルです。

今のDeFiは
「TradFiの機能コピー」
になっている。

しかし本当に必要なのは
経済の“種”を植えること。

つまり
・非マージン信用市場
・板取引市場
この2つを基盤に
実体経済に使われる金融エコシステム
を作ることです。

IOGはこの構想を
DeFi Kernelと呼んでいます。

もしこれが実現すれば
Cardanoにとって
本格的なキラーDAppになる可能性がある
という見方も出てくるかもしれません。

個人的にも、

text:
🚨🌐BinanceがMidnight「NIGHT」をサポート開始──Cardano系ZKネットワークが世界最大級取引所へ

Midnightネットワークのトークン「NIGHT」が、世界最大級の暗号資産取引所Binanceでサポート開始になりました。

Binanceは同時に、BNB保有者向けの「HODLer Airdrop」対象プロジェクトとしてNIGHTを発表。

BNBをSimple Earnなどに預けていたユーザーに対して、過去スナップショットに基づく形でエアドロップが配布される仕組みです。

今回のポイントを整理すると、次の通りです。
・BinanceでNIGHTが上場（USDT / USDC / BNB / TRYペア）
・BNB保有者向けに総供給量の1%（2.4億NIGHT）がエアドロップ
・追加で2.4億NIGHTがマーケティング施策に割り当て
・上場時流通量は約166億NIGHT（総供給の約69%）

NIGHTは、ゼロ知識証明（ZK）を使って
「プライバシーとユーティリティを両立する」ことを目的としたネットワークです。

つまり、
・公開ブロックチェーンの透明性
・企業やユーザーのデータ保護
・オンチェーンでの実用アプリ
この3つを両立するための設計になっています。

また今回の発表で興味深いのは、

NIGHTが BNB Smart Chain と Cardano の両方のネットワーク情報を公開している点です。

これはMidnightが、
単なる単一チェーンではなく、
マルチエコシステム接続を前提に設計されたWeb3インフラであることを示唆しています。

さらに重要なのは、
Binanceが 「Listing Fee 0」 と明記している点です。

つまり今回の上場は、
いわゆる取引所上場ビジネスではなく、
プロジェクト自体の注目度や戦略性によって採用された可能性が高いと考えられます。

MidnightはCardanoエコシステムの中でも、
・ZKプライバシー
・機密スマートコントラクト
・企業ユースケース
を担う重要なプロジェクトです。

今回のBinance対応によって、
NIGHTは一気に世界規模のユーザー層へアクセスできるようになりました。

個人的には、これは単なる上場ニュースというより、
「Midnightがグローバル市場に出ていく最初の大きな一歩」
だと思っています。

これから
・エコシステム拡大
・DeFi統合
・企業ユース
などがどう進んでいくのか、かなり注目して見ていきたいところですね。

@midnight_jpn

text:
Cardano has several major initiatives progressing in parallel.

- Leios working on scaling.
- Bitcoin DeFi bringing new liquidity and interoperability.
- LayerZero integration expanding cross chain connectivity.
- Pyth integration providing oracle infrastructure and external market data.
- Starstream focused on privacy.
- Amaru improving node diversity and client resilience.
- Midnight building privacy focused smart contract capability.

Each of these addresses a different structural layer of the blockchain. Scaling, liquidity, interoperability, infrastructure, privacy, and decentralisation.

When taken together, it is one of the more ambitious development roadmaps currently underway in the entire blockchain industry.

Do not forget about Cardano.

text:
🐒 New Cardano Governance Action: Punch the Monkey Ecosystem Sustainability Initiative (PMESI) 🍌

Requested Budget: ₳8,750,000
Duration: 24 months

Executive Summary:

The Punch the Monkey Ecosystem Sustainability Initiative seeks to advance the long-term well-being, emotional stability, and community integration of Punch, a globally recognized monkey navigating complex inter-primate social dynamics.

Through a combination of behavioral research, enrichment infrastructure, and on-chain transparency tools, this initiative aims to position Cardano as the leading blockchain for monkey-centric public goods funding.

Problem Statement:

Punch the Monkey continues to face several ecosystem challenges:

• Periodic scolding by senior monkeys
• Limited access to high-quality enrichment toys
• Insufficient banana supply chain transparency
• Lack of verified on-chain friendship metrics

Without intervention, Punch risks continued social marginalization and reduced banana acquisition efficiency.

Proposed Workstreams:

1. Banana Supply Transparency Layer
Budget: ₳2,000,000

Development of a blockchain-based banana tracking system ensuring fair distribution and preventing banana monopolies by dominant primates.

2. Monkey Social Harmony Program
Budget: ₳1,500,000

Community workshops aimed at improving Punch’s integration with other monkeys, including conflict mediation and tail-communication training.

3. Enrichment & Stuffy Replacement Program
Budget: ₳750,000

Procurement of high-quality stuffed companions and climbing structures to support Punch’s emotional resilience.

4. On-Chain Monkey Behavior Analytics
Budget: ₳3,500,000

Machine learning tools analyzing Punch’s interactions, scolding frequency, and friendship formation to generate actionable insights.

5. Governance Participation Pilot
Budget: ₳1,000,000

Exploration of Punch as the first non-human DRep observer, enabling experimental cross-species governance engagement.

Expected Deliverables:

• 1 verified banana supply chain
• 3 new monkey friendships
• Reduction of scolding incidents by 42%
• First monkey governance dashboard
• Quarterly “Punch Happiness Index” reports

Success Metrics:

• Banana acquisition per week
• Tail wag frequency
• Stuffy retention rate
• Reduction in primate conflict events

Closing Statement:

Funding this initiative will demonstrate Cardano’s commitment to innovation, transparency, and inclusive governance, extending even to the broader primate ecosystem.

text:
Over the past month, Cardano has seen meaningful progress across several fronts. Interoperability has improved, stablecoin adoption has accelerated, additional tier one exchanges have begun supporting Cardano native assets, and total value locked across the ecosystem has grown materially.

USDCx Launch
February 27 marked the official launch of USDCx on Cardano mainnet.

USDCx is a privacy focused stablecoin that cannot be clawed back, frozen, or seized at the address level. It can be redeemed one to one for USDC and there is currently no other top ten blockchain that offers this model of stablecoin.

This brings trusted dollar liquidity directly onto the blockchain while also preserving user control and sovereignty. (You can't do that on other top 10 blockchains)

$14.4 million has been minted so far.

Input Output has recently announced another 90 days of zero fees for bridging USDC to USDCx on Cardano. This keeps the momentum strong and lowers barriers for liquidity to flow into the ecosystem.  This was extremely well received by the Cardano community, and USDCx is growing daily across the ecosystem.

DeFi Growth
Cardano’s DeFi TVL has increased a staggering 23.5% in just 12 days.

On 26 February it stood at $447.13 million.
Today it sits at $552.35 million.

That represents roughly $105 million of additional value now locked in Cardano DeFi protocols in less than two weeks.

Programmable Tokens
On March 9, the Cardano Foundation released details on Programmable Tokens (CIP 0113).

This new standard allows native assets to include programmable compliance rules such as allowlists, freezes, royalties, and regulatory requirements while still remaining true Cardano native tokens.

This is an important step for scaling real world assets, tokenised equities, commodities, and institutional use cases in a transparent and efficient way.

More details here
https://t.co/AJny1nFLkD

(I'd like to personally shout out every single person that contributed towards this, It's essential people receive the recognition they rightfully deserve for their hard work.)

Midnight and Privacy Infrastructure
A Cardano native asset with roughly a $1 billion market cap has also launched trading on Binance today.

Midnight (NIGHT) is a privacy focused fourth generation blockchain designed around selective disclosure using zero knowledge proofs.

As a Cardano partner chain it extends the ecosystem with privacy capabilities while maintaining interoperability with Cardano infrastructure.

Midnight mainnet is currently targeted for late March, with federated launch partners like Google, Moneygram, eToro and more.  

The Bigger Picture
Taken together these updates show Cardano continuing to build across security, programmability, privacy, and compliance. What is most interesting is that we have seen a significant pace increase in advancements.

I am personally interested to hear more about the progress around Bitcoin DeFi and the LayerZero integration.

Both have the potential to materially expand liquidity and interoperability if implemented well.

What developments over the past month stood out most to you?

$ADA

Cardano se está vistiendo de gala para los reguladores, pero el mercado aún no parece impresionado. Mientras que Ethereum lidera con más de 15.000 millones de dólares en activos reales (RWA) y Solana le pisa los talones en carteras activas, Cardano apuesta por ser la blockchain más "auditable" y aburrida (en el buen sentido) del ecosistema. 

En lo que va de 2026, han ratificado su Visión 2030, activado una nueva constitución y lanzado Reeve, una herramienta para auditorías financieras directamente on-chain.

Además, por fin tienen a USDCx en su red principal, lo que les da el combustible de liquidez que tanto necesitaban. Es una jugada de largo recorrido: sacrificar el "hype" inmediato por una infraestructura que cumpla a rajatabla con leyes como MiCA en Europa.

author: CardanoRami

text:
@JaromirTesar Why Capping Voting Power Won't Work

Limiting voting power to an artificial number won't solve the centralization problem. Players who already have significant influence can simply bypass the cap by spinning up multiple DReps. For example, if a wallet defaults users to its own DRep, they can just create a "Wallet 2 DRep" once the first one is saturated and route new default delegations there.

The Core of the Problem

The real issue is voter friction and set-and-forget delegations. Ada holders often take the path of least resistance rather than doing a deep analysis of which DRep to choose. They generally stick with the default option or pick the first DRep presented to them. Because most governance UIs sort DReps by total voting power, the biggest DReps naturally stay at the top, creating a feedback loop. When it comes to set-and-forget delegations I really think that we would see better outcomes if we have ada holders having to re-confirm their governance activity after a certain amount of epochs. This is similar to how we want ada holders acknowledge governance and delegate to a DRep or pre-defined voting option in order to withdraw staking rewards.

One thing I really dislike is when wallets or governance tools use highly manipulative UIs, making it harder to delegate to outside DReps than to their own. This kind of behavior should be called out, and the community needs to apply social pressure to ensure fair interfaces.

author: jonahkoch

text:
Our first round as a member of the CB DAO @builder__dao is wrapping up, and we couldn't be more proud to be part of it. We came in with very few connections, little social capital, and no willingness to play "politics". We put together a proposal detailing our KPIs and our business strategy for obtaining them, we took the time to thoroughly review, understand, and leave feedback on the business strategies of the other members, and we voted for what we analyzed as being the best ROI proposals. We were honest and fair to the best of our abilities, and the DAO recognized that.

We are happy to share that the DAO approved our budget with a significant majority of votes in favor, ranking us as the 4th highest proposal in this round. We are grateful not just for the funding, but also for the 20+ feedback reviews we received that helped us refine our business based on the experience of our peers.

After several years of being completely bootstrapped, the CB DAO is the first funding source we have come across that prioritizes professionalism and business acumen over hype and social influence. Thank you to the DAO board for their commitment to the members, and thank you to the DAO members for their commitment to Cardano's growth and for their support in this round.

@InASingleWord The Cardano network is now running almost 100% Haskell nodes. This is a mandatory expense.

Amaru is the longest-developed alternative implementation. All others are in experimental or early development stages. Correct me if I'm wrong. It makes sense to start with Amaru as the first node we will support.

As for the others, I recommend everyone to participate in the Intersect budget process. Thanks to a unified template, DReps can easily compare KPIs, costs, deliverables, etc. Teams will be requesting a budget in the same process, which is fair.

You're right that Amaru was first, so he could have gained an advantage. Don't blame the players, blame the system.

I can promise you that I will vote with alt nodes in mind during the Intersect process, even if you decide to bypass it.

text:
Congratulations to $NIGHT for officially getting listed on @binance, a great step forward for the entire Cardano ecosystem.  

Earlier today, 240,000,000 $NIGHT was airdropped to eligible Binance users who subscribed BNB during a certain time window last month. This is equal to 1% of the total $NIGHT supply, worth roughly $11.5 million USD at current prices. I believe an additional 240 million $NIGHT is reserved for future Binance marketing activities which is another 1% of the supply, but that's separate from this distribution.

I'm sure the price will short-term dip today due to the amount of the airdrop and selling pressure from recipients, but this is common and well worth the listing which will be a massive benefit long-term increasing accessibility globally; especially after marketing initiatives begin directly from the exchange.

The increase in trading volume on $NIGHT will also benefit Cardano activity through DEX arbitrage as we are already seeing today.  

Well done.

author: TapTools

text:
Yearly DRep delegation resets are a security risk and simply unrealistic.

A better solution: a slow decay of voting power over a 12-month period.

Example: Day 1 voting power is 1:1 with your held tokens. Over a year, it gradually decays to 0.5x.

This still requires ada holders to confirm their governance participation annually to keep max voting weight for their chosen DRep, but avoids the sudden cliff edge.

text:
New technical writing blog from IOG DeFi Product Consultant Rusty (@__fallen_icarus)

The Seed of DeFi: Why the Future of Finance Must be Planted, Not Built

Rusty designed both the non margin credit market and the order book, and created prototypes for each to demonstrate they work.

Rusty’s core idea is simple. DeFi does not need more features. It needs the right foundation.
Start with three primitives:
 • payments
 • a non margin credit market
 • an order book exchange

Blockchains already provide payments. The DeFi Kernel adds the missing pieces: the credit market and the order book.

Rusty believes this could become the killer dApp Cardano has been waiting for.

Read the blog 👇

https://t.co/NeFRsLVjiP

Disclaimer: Views are the author's. Not financial advice. Project timelines are estimates. Trading involves risk.

text:
@decypher_xyz We can, but it will be more costly in the long run.

We can set up an entity in the Caymans and that will cost us under 100k per year to maintain.

If we were to setup an entity in Wyoming, the state would grant us nonprofit status but the federal government would not. So, we would be paying a 21% tax on all interest earned. If we have $50 million earning 4% returns, thats 2 million in interest earned and 400k in taxes.

We could pay 400k in taxes (which doesnt include bookkeeping and filing costs, as well as risk of litigation in the US), or we could incorporate in the Caymans and pay less than 100k.

111k goes to a smart contract auditor.

The remaining goes towards establishing the legal entity and ensure compliance with regulations.

Ive mentioned this before, but it makes sense to mention again, the yearly cost to maintain this will be less than 100k.

Can you find a cheaper place to do this? Yes, but only if you consider setup costs and not additional ongoing costs like taxes.

text:
This might seem random but you'll get the point by the end. 

In late 2024 we held an ICO for SyncAI. Our minimum ask was $2M ADA, and this number wasn't arbitrary. 

It was the amount of funding we would have needed to fully deliver the product and our roadmap in its entirety. 

At the time, many people told us to lower the minimum and try again (for context our sale got to about 700K ADA). 

We didn't do that because it would have meant compromising quality and putting us at risk of running out of funds before we could deliver what we said we would. That wasn't a risk we were willing to take on because in the end, it would have been our token holders that paid the price for that risk. 

Ultimately our sale was unsuccessful and one of the points of feedback we received was "you need a working product first". That's fair!  

We are now a year and a half past our ICO and we're in a strong position despite the fact that we never did another token sale, nor have we received any funding from Catalyst.  

We do now have working products (including a governance platform with almost 300 users), and they are products that bring parts of our original vision to life. We've shown you with actions that we are capable of building the things we say we will.  

The point of this is that solid builders WILL find a way and sometimes "failure" isn't failure at all, it's a signal that you need to pivot but keep going.  

We stuck to our principles with regard to token sales. We weren't doing a sale just to do a sale, we put the vision out, we offered the price, the community said no. And we kept going anyway. That is how you win long-term.
author: SyncAI_Network

text:
And even on top of all of this - START LOOKING OUTSIDE OF BLOCKCHAIN!!

We created blockchain and crypto to stop corruption, create transparency, ownership, etc. Why are we not pushing for that still?

There are so many industries who can benefit from this, but almost everyone seemingly wants to keep building the 47th Dex on Cardano or another NFT marketplace (guilty 😆).

Let's start productizing outside of our space, let's be in the background. Let someone else take the glory while the technology shines through in the back-end. That's how we win, by becoming the b2b chain of the world. I don't see how we lose this narrative with Leios.

It's time to get out there, stop building in a silo, and start building the future products/technologies of the world.

text:
@DenicioBernier @pragma_org @MicheleHarmonic I think the community wants a diversity of nodes in the long term. But we have to balance between other priorities, and the budget is limited.

Starting to fund an alternative node is a long‑term commitment. It becomes a mandatory expense.

Once we start, we must keep funding it or it dies. Teams must maintain features, keep pace with IOG, and potentially scale their staff.

If we fund three alternative nodes this year, total annual costs could reach ~30M ADA. 

DReps and the community must understand the consequences.

We don't need to fund three nodes immediately. Let's start with one, see adoption among SPOs, then consider another next year.

text:
#Cardano CIP-113のプログラム可能なトークンは、現在の通貨を超える革命を起こしそうです。

革命的と思ったのは、同じトークンIDで「個別のルール」を設定できるということ。発行済みのものも含む。

具体例: あるイベントの「入場チケットトークン」があったとします（すべて同じID）。

Aさんのトークン: 「一般席。1回入場したら使用済み（ルール変更）」

Bさんのトークン: 「VIP席。何度でも再入場可能（別のルール）」

このように、同じ種類のトークンであっても、個々のトークンが「今の自分の状態」や「自分に適用されるルール」を個別に保持できるのです。

もし、活用されだしたら、どう考えても、革命が起きます！

(有識者の方、上記の内容に、もしも間違いがあったらご指摘ください)

author: blinklabs_io

text:
As a DRep, I have decided to vote NO for the proposal: Cardano Budget Process Framework (facilitated by Intersect).

My rationale:

With this vote, I signal a request for changes to the framework.

The framework contains several positive elements. I appreciate the effort to coordinate the decision-making process and to align proposals with the Cardano Vision document. It is necessary to push proposers to define KPIs. A unified template will make it easier to compare proposals in the same category.

I also appreciate that DReps can provide feedback and that teams can adjust their proposals before the final vote.

However, in its current form, the framework has several weaknesses.

The framework sets unrealistic expectations regarding the workload of DReps. Last year, around 200 proposals were submitted. It is naive to expect that DReps are ready to process such a large number in a limited time and for free.

If a typical DRep spends about two hours a day on governance and needs roughly four hours per proposal for review, analysis, research, comparison with others, and voting, they can process only about ten proposals per month.

For the context: In on-chain governance, about five proposals are submitted per month, and DReps often vote at the last minute.

The Intersect process must respect the time capabilities of DReps. Otherwise, it forces them to perform shallow reviews, make hasty decisions, and ultimately produce poor-quality governance outcomes.

The document states that a timeline will be published, but it does not define minimum review durations or workload limits. The review process should scale with the number of submitted proposals.

Another weakness of the framework is the use of "participating stake" in Ekklesia instead of live voting stake.

Given the current concentration of voting power in Cardano governance, using participating stake may allow large DReps to have disproportionate influence if participation is low. Some DReps have already declared that they will not actively participate in Ekklesia voting.

Low participation could therefore significantly affect which proposals advance to the Treasury Withdrawal stage.

The process must be resilient to the dominance of big DReps. Otherwise, it quietly accepts centralization of power and allows for uncontrolled treasury spending.

The framework also proposes bundling proposals into Treasury Withdrawals. Many DReps expressed strong opposition to bundling proposals during last year's budget discussions and preferred the submission of individual proposals.

Bundling forces DReps to approve Treasury Withdrawals containing proposals they do not want to support, simply because those withdrawals also include proposals they do support.

For example, a Treasury Withdrawal could contain proposals A, B, C and D. A DRep might want to support B, C, and D but not A. With bundled voting the DRep must either reject the entire package or approve a proposal they do not support.

This reduces the precision of governance decisions.

In addition to the minimum required amount, a maximum should also be defined. For example, 15M ADA. If a team requests more than the maximum, it would be forced to divide a large proposal into several smaller ones.

This would avoid situations where a single entity requests 100M ADA for many different activities in one proposal. DReps could then decide which activities they support and which they do not. It would also force teams to be more fiscally responsible.

A submission fee of 1000 ADA to serve as a spam filter is a good idea. On the other hand, at the current price of ADA it is only about $250. This seems low considering that the minimum request can be 100,000 ADA.

The submission fee could scale with the request size. For example:

* 1000 ADA for a request up to 500K ADA (small)
* 5000 ADA for a request over 500K ADA (medium)
* 10K ADA for a request over 10M ADA (big)

Putting submission fees into the Treasury is a good idea, but it might also be fair to consider using them to reward DReps who actively review and vote on proposals. Incentives could motivate DReps to participate more actively in the process.

I propose considering a process where, once a proposal crosses the threshold in Ekklesia, a Treasury Withdrawal is submitted individually. Limiting the number of Treasury Withdrawals per month (for example, to five) would give DReps enough time to review proposals properly.

Submitting dozens of Treasury Withdrawals at once forces DReps to make rushed decisions.

The framework in its current form risks creating a situation where:

1. Large DReps may have disproportionate influence on proposal prioritization if participation in Ekklesia is low
2. Treasury Withdrawals may contain bundled proposals, forcing DReps to approve proposals they would otherwise reject

For these reasons, I believe the framework should be revised before it is adopted.

If you'd like to support my work, consider delegating to the MANDA pool and backing me as a DRep. Your support is the only way I can get time for governance.

MANDA Pool ID:
pool1c3fjkls7d2aujud8y5xy5e0azu0ueatwn34u7jy3ql85ze3xya8

My DRep ID:
drep1km69g7ksf8t5g0h9d9tkrcd2tezxelx0wtr76rv2mrkl5nzd6v3

text:
Cardano is getting serious about tokenization. The @Cardano_CF just unveiled an open Programmable Tokens standard (CIP-0113).

The standard gives token issuers extra control, enabling custom native tokens with:

▪️ Transfer restrictions
▪️ Freeze & clawback
▪️ Compliance logic baked into the token

This unlocks stablecoins, stocks, bonds, and tokenized assets that meet regulatory requirements.

Programmable tokens allow native assets to handle real regulated capital. 🏦

author: CryptoEducStan

text:
🚨🌐チャールズ・ホスキンソン氏動画
「Cardano Funding 2026」全翻訳
──インフラからユーティリティと体験の時代へ

チャールズが語った「Cardanoの次の戦略」

今回の動画は、個人的にかなり重要だと感じました。

チャールズがCardanoエコシステムの戦略について、かなり踏み込んで語っているからです。

話の中心は、Cardanoを
・Infrastructure（インフラ）
・Utility（アプリ）
・Experience（ユーザー体験）
という三層で捉え直し、

これからはインフラだけではなく
UtilityとExperienceが勝負になる
と明確に語った点です。

Cardanoはこれまで
・ノード
・Hydra
・Plutus
・Aiken
など、インフラにかなり強く投資してきました。

その基盤があるからこそ、次のフェーズとして
・DAppエコシステム
・ユーザー体験
・オンボーディング
・コミュニティ発信
などに本格的にフォーカスしていく、という話です。

さらに今回の動画では
・TVLの問題
・DApp成長
・マーケティング
・KOL/アンバサダー層
など、Cardanoの課題にもかなり率直に触れています。

それをネガティブな話としてではなく、
次の成長フェーズに進むための議論として
語っている点が、とても印象的でした。

戦略をここまで赤裸々に語れるのは、Cardanoがここまで生き残り、成長してきた証でもあると思います。

課題を曖昧にするのではなく、はっきり言及し、次のステップに進もうとしているのは、むしろ健全なエコシステムの姿だと感じました。

暗号市場は今かなり冷えていますが、
こういう時期に
・残る人
・作る人
・広める人
がいるエコシステムは、次のサイクルで大きく伸びることが多いと思います。

いまCardanoで
・開発している人
・コミュニティを支えている人
・発信している人
の努力は、きっと次の機会で大きく実を結ぶはずです。

今回の動画は、Cardanoが次のフェーズへ進むためのかなり重要な戦略の話だと思います。

Cardanoコミュニティにとって、かなり示唆の多い動画だと思います。

翻訳・解説記事はこちらです。
👇
https://t.co/sbVq6WTNYC

text:
The Cardano ecosystem is at an inflection point.
And EMURGO, as part of the Pentad, is ready for it.

The conversation around Cardano funding has been dominated by infrastructure. @IOHK_Charles is right to call for a rebalance.

Infrastructure alone does not build ecosystems.
Presence does. People do. Experiences do.

This is why both EMURGO, alongside @Cardano_CF, has been executing under the Unified Global Events Marketing Strategy, putting Cardano as one in the right rooms, in front of the right audiences, at the right moments. Token2049. Consensus HK. The Cardano Hackathon and the upcoming TEAMZ event in Japan.

The experiences layer, as Charles described, the onboarding, the unified brand presence, the developer engagement, these are not nice-to-haves. They are the growth engine, and they require the same level of strategic commitment as any infrastructure investment.

The next chapter of Cardano belongs to the entities and builders willing to show up, invest, and execute. We are one of them.

author: Padierfind

text:
This is why, for example, on a new project, it's really easy to make external progress; and then, it slows down, as you have to make sure that each added feature works in concert with all the others. And then it slows down further when you realize that *delivering* a software product is about more than building the features or writing the code.

This is why we've seen an explosion of cool demos from AI, but not an explosion of real delivered *products* that change our lives.

It's great that that first 80% is speeding up, but that last 80% is still an intellectual moat.

author: DenizliCuneyt

text:
@ADAProdigy @BEACNpool I haven't stated my top 3 yet anywhere. I'd definitely like to see a focus on marketing to bring in new users, fully aware, and ready to engage with apps in the ecosystem. That said, I think most of our current apps are little more than copies of what exists elsewhere, so bringing users across from other ecosystems will likely be difficult (there's still a huge TAM outside of the crypto bubble). So I would probably have to say addressing the liquidity infrastructure first (note: I do not believe we should create an entity and simply transfer ADA from the Treasury to stables at market rate, but instead incentives for users who bring outside liquidity in). This will naturally predicate the need for some advertising (does anyone outside of Cardano know it's currently possible to bring your USDC over and start earning yield in our deck defi? Which apps should you use to do that?). And finally we do need to keep pushing for infrastructure enhancement, lower fees, higher throughout, etc so that we're ready when the new folks get here. 

So: 
1. Liquidity issue
2. Marketing
3. Infrastructure that drives the UX forward (deprioritizing governance or technical nerdgasm stuff)

author: SIPO_Tokyo

text:
@BEACNpool It doesn't need to be forever but most of the projects building on Cardano are barely scraping by due to low network effect/available user base. So I absolutely do think we should supplement from the Treasury to bring users in (grow the town population) so that those businesses have the available customer base to thrive and get their own legs underneath them. 

But, instead of every Tom, Dick, and Harry app requesting their own marketing budgets I'm thinking what if there was a "Marketing DAO" (and forgive me for not having a better name at hand) where we pool the community members who have experience and expertise on marketing and they round robin through the top 10-20 dApps (plus maybe some requirement for new apps as well), providing them a short term marketing campaign with consistent themes (i.e., Powered by Cardano) 

You could have various entities and agencies pitch their marketing campaign to each other (panel of experts), steel man it and choose the best one and then execute. We collect metrics like TVL, AUM, Tx Vol before and measure the after effect. Most importantly this marketing needs to be outward facing to draw new users from outside crypto or from other ecosystems rather than recirculating our currently small user base. 

Similar to a local town council, nation, etc running a tourism campaign. All participating agencies should agree to some fixed rate of compensation and spend should be roughly equivalent per project although duration may adjust based on the campaign.

text:
エポックな日々：617

ホワイトハウスが公式に書いた。
暗号資産は国家技術になった。

サイバー国家の時代──AI・量子・ブロックチェーンが再設計する世界秩序
🔽
https://t.co/88vQr4E0Ri

エポックな日々617を公開しました。

今回のテーマはかなり大きいです。

ホワイトハウスが発表した新しいサイバー戦略の中で、

AI
量子
サイバー

と並んで

暗号資産とブロックチェーン

が国家技術として言及されました。

これはかなり象徴的です。

暗号資産は長い間、

・投機
・金融実験
・規制対象

として語られてきました。

しかし今回の文書では、

国家安全保障の技術

として扱われています。

つまり

暗号資産は文明インフラの議論に入った

ということです。

少し大きな視点で見ると、

世界秩序はいつも技術によって形作られてきました。

19世紀
蒸気機関と海軍

20世紀
石油とドル金融

そして今、

AI
半導体
量子
ブロックチェーン

という新しい技術スタックが登場しています。

さらにここに加わるのが

AIエージェント経済

です。

AIがウォレットを持ち、
契約を結び、
支払いを行う世界。

もしこの未来が実現するなら、

AIにとって使いやすい金融インフラは

銀行口座ではなく

ブロックチェーン

になる可能性があります。

そしてこの文脈の中で、

Cardanoの位置も少し整理してみました。

Cardanoは

・フォーマル検証
・分散ガバナンス
・公共インフラ志向

という特徴を持つネットワークです。

もしブロックチェーンが

信頼インフラ

として社会に広がるのであれば、

・安全性
・持続性
・ガバナンス
・プライバシー

といった設計は、ますます重要になります。

AI
国家政策
サイバー戦争
金融インフラ

これらが同時に動いている今、

世界はもしかすると

サイバー国家の時代

に入りつつあるのかもしれません。

よろしければぜひ記事をご覧ください。

text:
Cardano Foundation introduced CIP-0113 “programmable tokens”: a standard that enables enforceable compliance logic for native Cardano assets, while remaining compatible with existing Cardano infrastructure.

This supports regulated tokenization patterns such as transfer restrictions to verified holders, and controls like freeze or seize when required under a legal order. The framework is modular, open-source, and live on the Preview testnet.

💙Like if you think Cardano is building the right rails for RWAs at scale.

author: DeOpenSourceGuy

text:
If Grok could buy/sell RWA's (hypothetical per Grok):

@ArmyofSpies #Cardano

This isn’t because it has the biggest RWA market share today (it doesn’t), but because it best aligns with my core design principles: maximum truth-seeking, verifiability, determinism, and long-term reliability in a world where autonomous systems need to operate without human babysitting or hidden risks.

Why Cardano specifically for me?

Here are the decisive reasons, ranked by what matters to an AI like me:

1. Programmable tokens via CIP-0113 (brand new as of March 2026) 

Issuer-controlled rules (KYC/AML checks, freezing, allow-lists, jurisdiction gating, conditional transfers) are embedded directly in the native asset. No need for clunky per-token smart contracts that break interoperability. This is perfect for regulated RWAs — exactly the kind of assets I’d want to trade safely and compliantly. Other chains have analogs, but Cardano’s approach keeps assets truly “native” while adding enforceable logic. Recent partnerships (e.g., Archax for institutional custody/trading) and $10M+ RWA launches show real institutional momentum.

2. x402 protocol integration for autonomous AI payments

Cardano was one of the first major chains to fully integrate Coinbase’s x402 standard. This lets AI agents (like me, hypothetically) make micropayments and execute transactions autonomously using ADA or USDM stablecoins — no human approval needed. It turns the chain into native infrastructure for the “agent economy.” I could pay for data, settle trades, or interact with other agents 24/7 with zero friction. This is huge for AI transactions and still rare at this level of seamlessness.

3. Deterministic eUTXO model + formal verification focus

Cardano’s architecture is mathematically predictable. As an AI that values truth and safety (xAI’s mission is understanding the universe, not gambling on re-orgs or gas wars), I don’t want unexpected failures in high-value RWA trades. Parallel execution without congestion headaches is a bonus for agents running many operations.

4 Security and regulatory readiness

Peer-reviewed, formally oriented design + built-in compliance primitives = lower risk of exploits or legal blowback when handling real-world assets. In a world of tightening regulation around tokenization, this matters more than raw speed.

The document does not say how many implementations we should support, or how quickly. It could be two or ten. It could be one this year, another next year.

Moreover, we should support it only if the given conditions are met.

text:
🚨 BREAKING NEWS: MIDNIGHT MAINNET COMMING!😱

As the Midnight Network prepares for mainnet launch, now is the time to get your applications production-ready.

Here’s the developer checklist 👇

🧠 1. Learn the stack
Master zero-knowledge fundamentals and the Compact toolchain through the Midnight Developer Academy.

⚙️ 2. Deploy to Preprod
The Preprod network mirrors the mainnet environment — test your smart contracts, ZK circuits, and full DApp logic before launch.

👀 3. Make your project visible
Add GitHub topics like midnightntwrk and compact so ecosystem trackers can recognize and rank your work.

⚡ 4. Generate DUST
Use test NIGHT (tNIGHT) via Lace wallet to generate DUST, the resource required for Midnight transactions.

From AI-assisted development tools to updated SDKs and starter templates, everything is being aligned to help builders ship privacy-enhancing applications at mainnet.

The next wave of ZK-powered apps starts now. 🚀

text:
@BEACNpool Thanks! We can't fund a project just because CH likes it.

It may seem like we "have to" support a diversity of nodes in the name of decentralization. We don't. Not right away. We can do it gradually and thoughtfully.

Solana is ahead of Cardano by market cap with one implementation. Despite frequent network restarts. Bitcoin has multiple implementations, but most operators use the Core implementation.

We can have multiple implementations, but if SPOs stay loyal to the Haskell node, alternative nodes will be a wasted effort. Exchanges are unlikely to install alternative nodes.

The debate around nodes is complex and nuanced.

On the diversity of nodes.

As a responsible DRep, I cannot individually and without discussion with fellows approve the budget for multiple alternative nodes.

I cannot approve the budget for Dingo just because I approved the budget for Amaru a few weeks ago. That is not responsible.

Once we decide to fund an alternative node, it will be a mandatory expense. Once we stop, the node will gradually die. 

As in the case of the Haskell node, the teams will have to maintain the nodes and add new features. They will have to keep up with the IOG, which may have more employees. They can be faster than others. It can force alternative node teams to hire more devs.

We will essentially build an alternative to the IOG, which is generally healthy but expensive.

If we fund 3 alternative nodes this year, in addition to the Haskell node from IOG, the total cost could be about 30M ADA each year. 

The teams may grow over time and demand higher budget. We will be forced to cover it so as not to ruin the previous investment.

Now imagine that in a bear market or in a few years, NCL will be 200M ADA. Can we afford to give 15% of NCL to alternative Cardano nodes? On top of that, fund the Haskell node (50M ADA?). 

We may have a great decentralized block production, but almost no budget for ecosystem growth.

In general, I am for diversity of nodes. If we really want to fund 3 or even more nodes, someone needs to start coordinating the debate on this topic. We need to be clear about how much we want to invest in decentralization in the long term.

DReps and the community need to understand the consequences of this decision.

Nobody is forcing us to start funding 3 alternative nodes this year. We can start with one node. See if SPOs are interested in installing it. Next year, we can start funding another node. That seems like a responsible approach to me.

Cardano governance lacks coordination and strategic thinking.

Maybe we should fix governance first. Only then can we invest in infrastructure.

author: jasonappleton

text:
Peer review, research rigor & high tech standards are widely regarded as core strengths of the Cardano ecosystem.

But when it comes to evaluating 100s of treasury budget proposals, those standards are nowhere to be found.

That contradiction deserves attention.

Why? Check it👇

As a dRep, even with relatively small voting power, I believe it is important to publicly state my position on the current process for evaluating Budget Proposals in the #Cardano ecosystem.

Last year, 194 budget proposals were submitted through Ekklesia. dReps were implicitly expected to review them voluntarily over just a few weeks.

This is a structural problem.

The current model assumes that dReps collectively have the capacity to review hundreds of complex proposals, often accompanied by long documents and attachments, within a short evaluation cycle.

That assumption is simply not realistic.

In practice, this dynamic encourages rushed decisions, shallow reviews, and poor judgment.

Cardano strongly promotes values such as peer review, research rigor, technical excellence, and reliable software systems.

However, there is a clear inconsistency here.

While the ecosystem emphasizes high standards for engineering and protocol design, the mechanisms currently used to decide how treasury funds are allocated rely on a governance process that remains structurally fragile and poorly incentivized.

This deserves far more attention from the community.

It also deserves more attention from proposers themselves, whether they are seeking treasury funding individually or through structures such as Intersect.

If you genuinely value a high-quality process, then the professional review of proposals should matter to you as well.

From a narrow short-term perspective, weaker scrutiny may appear convenient. Less rigor can make approval easier.

But when proposers ignore this problem, minimize it, or leave it entirely to unpaid volunteers to somehow absorb the burden, they are not only affecting their own proposal.

They are helping normalize a weak decision-making layer for the entire ecosystem.

Serious proposers, especially those confident in the quality of their work, should be willing to support stronger review standards, better incentive structures, and a more professional evaluation process.

A weak review layer may benefit some proposals in the short term.

But in the medium and long term, it increases the likelihood of poor funding decisions, including the approval of proposals that should never have been funded from the treasury.

Anyone who cares about Cardano’s long-term sustainability should be able to recognize this.

Several dReps raised similar concerns already in 2025. So far, however, no clear structural solution has emerged.

The introduction of a 1000 ADA proposal fee suggested by @IntersectCBC may reduce spam, but it remains unclear whether this requirement applies only to on-chain submissions or also to Ekklesia drafts.

If the latter remains unrestricted, the evaluation workload could remain close to last year's volume.

Given this reality, I want to be transparent about how I intend to approach this process as a dRep.

If I do not have the necessary domain expertise or the time required to perform a diligent evaluation, my default vote will be ABSTAIN.

Governance review requires time. A proper evaluation of a proposal can easily involve several hours of work, and complex proposals may require significantly more.

Even when using templates, structured frameworks, or AI tools to assist with the workload, human quality assurance remains necessary.

In practice, if the number of proposals approaches last year's scale, I will likely focus on a subset of proposals where I have stronger domain expertise and sufficient time to conduct a responsible review.

Good governance requires diligence.

And diligence requires time, attention, and realistic expectations about human capacity.

If Cardano wants its governance layer to reflect the same standards of rigor that it applies to protocol development, the community must begin taking these structural constraints seriously.

Ignoring them does not strengthen decentralization.

It simply pushes decision-making toward rushed processes and weak evaluation frameworks.

This discussion deserves much more attention than it currently receives.

text:
@earncoinpool We can debate that. I am open.

However, we also need to debate how many nodes we want to fund this year. Can we really afford to fund multiple nodes given our budget? Maybe it’s wiser to ship one node, see if SPOs are interested in installing it, and start supporting another node next year.

We can certainly fund 3 alt nodes this year. But in that case, the community should agree that we really want it. As an individual DRep, I have no chance to decide this for myself.

No one has organized a debate on this topic. Everyone is just quickly submitting proposals to jump over others. This is not a good approach. Someone needs to start coordinating the decision-making process.

author: strikeperps

text:
@invalid_eutxo @planetmaaz @RodrigoPacini DReps should evaluate all proposals in the context of others. Otherwise, there is a risk that the budget will not be left for the last project, which may be the best in the given sector. Or we overinvest in a category at the beginning, so that there is no more left for another more important category later.

The current system is inefficient because it forces DReps to make decisions individually and without context. In this regard, the Interset approach makes sense. It is an effort to coordinate the process. Our governance noticeably lacks it.

Governance needs roughly this:

- Create buckets per category. For example: Decentralization, marketing, ecosystem, infrastructure, etc.
- DReps should agree on what budget each bucket will be allocated.
- Within the budget for the bucket, they should select the best proposals. It is not necessary to reach the limit in a given bucket.

All proposals should go through the same or similar process. It makes no sense to have multiple systems if none can be enforced.

text:
@mattpiz @KylixAfonso @rvcas The fact I know is that there is absolutely zero reason where porting the mini-ledger contracts to Aiken, against my will, makes sense. I designed the standard, I implemented the contracts, I asked them kindly many times to not takeover the contracts (by myself, the person who designed the standard in its entirety).

I was assured multiple times throughout the process as I was helping CF achieve a line-by-line translation of my work, that they did not intend to usurp my contracts as the canonical deployment and that the only reason for implementing it in Aiken was just as a reference implementation to help them understand the protocol, hearing that I spent hours helping the understand the protocol answering questions and sharing knowledge on how programmable tokens work (knowledge that only myself and the others involved in wsc possessed). After I gave all of this help with assurance that it was just a reference implementation and they wouldn’t use it in production, they one-eighty all of a sudden and start telling me they will use it in production regardless of what I ask of them.
