text:
🚨🌐Weekly development report as of 2026-03-06
：決済導入から次世代研究まで広がる開発

Cardanoの最新「Cardanoエコシステム最新動向（2026/3/6）」が公開されました。

今週もエコシステム、技術、研究の各レイヤーで着実な進展が見られていますので、ポイントを整理してご紹介します。

まずエコシステム面では、実利用のニュースがいくつか出ています。

・スイスのSPAR 137店舗でADA決済が利用可能に
・Cardano Buidler Festが3月24〜25日にアルゼンチンで開催予定
・Bodega LabsがMidnight上で完全プライベート予測市場をローンチ予定
・DanogoがUSDCxレンディングプールを計画
・Cardano FoundationのReeve（Proof of Reservesツール）がBlockSignと統合予定

つまり、
「決済」「DeFi」「プライバシー」「証明インフラ」など、
エコシステムの実装レイヤーが同時に広がってきています。

■コア技術（Ledger）

台帳チームは今週、パフォーマンス改善と将来の拡張のための作業を進めました。

・台帳状態の再構成によるパフォーマンス改善
・Ledger HD実装の簡素化
・ネストされたトランザクション（nested transactions）のルール設計
・canonical ledger stateに新しい名前空間を追加
将来的なスケーリングや高度なトランザクション処理の土台づくりが進んでいる形です。

■ウォレット（Lace）

Laceチームは現在、Midnightメインネットのサポートを進めています。

さらに注目なのがLaceIDの開発です。

LaceIDは、
ウォレットの中で

・検証可能資格情報（Verifiable Credentials）
・NFT
・ネイティブ資産

を一緒に管理できるネイティブID機能になります。
現在はロードマップの第一段階である
Proof of Humanity（人間証明）フェーズが開発中。

つまりLaceは、
「ウォレット」→「デジタルIDハブ」へ進化していく方向です。

また、モバイルアプリ開発も継続中です。

■スケーリング（Mithril）

Mithrilチームは今週もかなり活発です。

主な進展は以下。

・SNARK検証キーの署名者認証を実装
・ロッタリー計算の精度調整
・SNARK回路リファクタリング
・再帰的Halo2回路の技術レポート作成
・SNARK事前集約プリミティブの開発
さらに
・ブロックとトランザクションの証明ルートをREST APIに実装
・次回リリースの準備
・Cardano node v10.6への対応

なども進行。

軽量クライアントや高速同期のための基盤が着実に整備されています。

■Voltaire（ガバナンス）

Intersect委員会選挙2026が発表されました。

立候補受付は
3月末から開始予定です。

Cardanoのオンチェーンガバナンス体制が、
さらに制度化されていくフェーズに入っています。

■研究（IOG Research）

今週は学術研究の発表もありました。
Financial Cryptography 2026で発表された論文：

① 地理的分散を促進するインセンティブモデル

ノードが特定地域に集中すると
・自然災害
・政治的規制
・攻撃
などのリスクが高まるため、

・地理的に不足している地域にノードを設置すると報酬
・RTT測定で実際の位置を検証

というゲーム理論モデルを提案。

② RestakingネットワークのSybil耐性

同じ担保を複数サービスに使うrestakingでは
「匿名化して罰則を回避する問題」
があり、研究では
すべての匿名攻撃を防ぐ完全なペナルティ設計は数学的に不可能
という結果が示されています。

かなり重要な示唆です。

■Midnight

さらに研究チームは
「査読済み研究を基盤とするMidnight」
というブログも公開。

Midnightは単なるプライバシーチェーンではなく、
学術研究ベースの設計思想で構築されていることが改めて強調されています。

今週のレポートを見ると、
・決済導入
・DeFi
・プライバシー
・ID
・軽量ノード
・ガバナンス
・学術研究
と、Cardanoのスタック全体が同時に前進しています。

Midnight、Mithril、LaceIDあたりは
2026年の重要テーマになりそうですね。

引き続き動向を追っていきたいと思います。

詳しくはこちらです。

author: AichiStakePool

text:
We voted

Governance Action:

📌 Net Change Limit of 300 Million ADA for Epochs 613–713

🗳️ Vote: 🔴 NO

A lower cap does not fix an underspecified budget design. The cap was reduced, but guardrails, guidelines & governance discipline are missing.

Full technical breakdown 👇

What is being voted on?

➡️ An Info Action to set a Net Change Limit (NCL) of 300,000,000 ADA for Epochs 613–713 (13 Feb 2026 → 3 Jul 2027). If approved, it supersedes any prior NCL for the same window and becomes the cap used to assess future Treasury Withdrawal actions.

More info on the Governance Action below 👇
🔗https://t.co/1blmVXIz7w

What did Agora evaluate?

🧾 The full on-chain text (Abstract, Motivation, Rationale) and what it omits

📉 The proposed cap vs 2025 treasury inflows claim (306,940,195 ADA) and the lack of verifiable sourcing

🧠 Structural governance risks: concentration, bundling, and weak coordination signals

⏱️ Review cadence risk: absence of cooldown / revision norms and how this impacts NCL credibility

📌 Whether this resubmission addresses gaps previously flagged in our NO vote on the prior NCL for the same period

🔹 Compared this proposal to the prior NCL assessment and rationale

🔹 Focused on whether any governance-quality improvements were introduced (they were not)

Why did we vote 🔴 NO?

🔼 Upsides

✅ Establishing an NCL is operationally necessary to keep Treasury Withdrawals constitutionally workable.

✅ 300M is closer to the prior year’s inflow figure than higher caps (on paper, it signals restraint).

🔽 Downsides

⚠️ Severely underspecified. The action provides no links, annexes, datasets, or reproducible methodology to validate the inflow reference it relies on.

⚠️ Does not address previously identified gaps. We already voted NO on the prior NCL proposed; this resubmission fails to fix the core issues (auditability, coordination guidance, governance discipline).

⚠️ No safeguards against concentration. A single aggregate cap without any accompanying public guidance enables large actors to capture most of the fiscal space again, as occurred last year.

⚠️ No anti-bundling posture. The proposal sets no expectations to discourage packaging heterogeneous work to force passage.

⚠️ No revision cadence discipline. Without a cooldown norm or defined exceptional triggers, the NCL remains easily revisitable, undermining its role as a credible coordination instrument.

⚠️ No acknowledgement of market externalities. Sell-pressure risk, USD-indexed budgeting behavior, and volatility buffers are ignored entirely.

⚠️ “NCL is just a parameter” is not an excuse for permanent under-specification.

If we keep approving NCLs as bare numbers with no accompanying non-binding coordination framework (allocation guidance, concentration norms, anti-bundling expectations), then where does this discussion ever happen?

We already had a full year after the last NCL. Nothing materialized. If “NCL is just a parameter” remains the default excuse, this will never be revisited, and the cap becomes a performative ceiling with no governance posture behind it.

I do not support the idea of setting a spending ceiling for 16–17 months while disregarding non-binding guidance on how that ceiling should be interpreted and coordinated. The NCL may be a parameter, but fiscal governance cannot be parameter-only forever.

Want to dig deeper?

📄 Full rationale:
🔗 https://t.co/1lFGMGU1hG

If independent, structured governance reviews resonate with you, delegate to @AgoraCardano.

text:
🚨🌐🌒Midnightメインネット目前：開発者数を“可視化”する3つのステップ

Midnightがいよいよメインネットに近づく中で、開発者コミュニティに向けた重要なアナウンスが出ています。

テーマはシンプルで、「開発者の活動を正しく可視化しよう」というものです。

現在、Midnightエコシステムにはすでに数百人規模の開発者が参加し、Compact言語での開発やPreprod環境でのアプリ構築が進んでいます。ただし、GitHubの設定やメタデータが正しく付与されていないと、外部の統計レポートにその活動がカウントされないという問題があります。

これがなぜ重要かというと、ブロックチェーン業界では

開発者数＝エコシステムの成長指標

として扱われることが多いからです。

特に、暗号資産業界で最も引用される開発者レポートを出しているのが Electric Capital です。

投資家、メディア、プロトコルなどが、このレポートを基に「どのチェーンが成長しているか」を判断しています。

Midnightはメインネット公開に向けて、初めてElectric Capitalにエコシステムの開発状況を提出するタイミングに入っています。

つまり今の数週間が、Midnightの「最初の開発者数」として歴史に記録される重要な期間ということです。

そこで開発者に呼びかけられているのが、次の3ステップです。

① GitHubに正しいトピックを追加

リポジトリのTopicsに
midnightntwrk（必須）
compact（Compact使用時）を追加する。これにより、統計ツールが「Midnight関連プロジェクト」と認識できるようになります。

② READMEに1行の帰属文を追加

例えば
「This project is built on the Midnight Network.」

のような文をREADME上部に入れることで、自動解析ツールがエコシステムとの関係を認識します。

③ Awesome dAppsリストにPRを送る

Midnightコミュニティが管理する
midnight-awesome-dapps

のリストに自分のプロジェクトを登録します。
これにより他の開発者やパートナーにも見つけてもらいやすくなります。

これらの作業は、1リポジトリあたり数分で完了する小さな作業ですが、
エコシステム全体の開発者数に直接反映される重要なアクションです。

ブロックチェーンの世界では、

「実際に開発されていること」だけでなく
「それがデータとして可視化されていること」

が非常に重要です。

Midnightはメインネット直前のこのタイミングで、
エコシステムの開発者活動を正式な形で世界に示そうとしているということですね。

開発者の方は、ぜひ自分のリポジトリを確認してみてください。

あなたのプロジェクトが、Midnightの最初の開発者統計にしっかり記録されるかもしれません。
👇
https://t.co/Ov48cC5DuZ

author: IntersectMBO

text:
@aaroncrosss I'm not sure I fully understand your question, but the way strategies work is this:

You place funds into the sundae order contract; part of that specifies what type of order: swap, deposit, withdrawal, etc.

One order type is "strategy", and instead of saying "swap 100 ADA for at least 90 of this token", it says "if someone, later, can present swap details signed by X, accept them."

The way we utilized that for the marketplace with demeter is that a demeter worker (small lightweight server) generates an ephemeral key; you put that in your strategy configuration when placing the order.

The worker then runs, monitors for the conditions you want, and when it hits whatever trigger condition is relevant to that specific strategy, it builds the relevant payload, signs it, and submits it to the decentralized scooper network. The scoopers all then include your order in the next batch, according to the order they received on-chain transactions and strategy executions.

So, dollar cost average is implemented as the worker waiting for your interval, then building a small payload that swaps only a portion of your funds (the order has 1000 ADA, but we swap 10 of it each week).

Trailing stop loss is implemented by first swapping to the target asset, then keeping track of a "floor price": initially X% below the current price, but each new pool update that raises the price raises the floor price.

If it ever sees a pool update that drops the price, its trigger condition is met, and it submits the sell order (with a configured slippage) on your behalf.

This mechanism is fairly flexible; and it's not tied to the marketplace, that's just one way we're making it easy for people to use. You can write and deploy your own strategies on your own hardware right now if you have the conviction and skills: 

https://t.co/Ac3ueBUccp

Let us know what other strategies you want to see, and we'll get them in the backlog!

text:
Thanks to Wanchain Bridge, Cardano had access to USDC and USDT even before the recent native minting of USDCx.

Let me explain the basic principles behind the Wanchain Bridge.

In general, blockchain bridges connect networks that do not natively interoperate. 

For example, a bridge can connect Cardano and Bitcoin, allowing a wrapped version of BTC to exist on Cardano.

A bridge must preserve asset value and prevent the same value from being spent simultaneously on multiple networks. 

When an asset is transferred from one blockchain to another, its spendable value should only exist in one place at any given time.

Each asset originates from a specific blockchain, called the original network. The bridge securely transfers the asset's value to another blockchain, known as the destination network, while ensuring the asset can later return to its original network.

Wanchain Bridge uses two main mechanisms to facilitate cross-chain transfers.

Lock–Mint / Burn–Unlock mechanism

This mechanism is used when transferring an asset to a blockchain where that asset does not exist natively.

Native coins such as BTC, ETH, or ADA cannot simply be created on another blockchain by a bridge. Instead, the bridge must lock the original asset on its native chain and mint a wrapped representation on the destination chain.

The process involves two pairs of operations:

▪️Lock–Mint
▪️Burn–Unlock

Imagine the following scenario: Alice sends USDC from Ethereum to Cardano.

First, the USDC tokens are locked in a bridge smart contract on Ethereum. Once Wanchain verifies the lock event (after waiting for several block confirmations), the bridge mints an equivalent amount of wrapped USDC on Cardano. Bob then receives the wrapped tokens.

The original USDC tokens remain locked on Ethereum until someone sends the wrapped USDC back to Cardano.

When that happens, the bridge performs the reverse process. 

The wrapped USDC tokens are first burned on Cardano. After the burn event is verified, the bridge unlocks the original native USDC tokens on Ethereum.

This ensures that the asset value exists in only one spendable form at a time.

Native-to-Native Lock–Unlock mechanism

In some cases, the same token already exists natively on multiple blockchains. In such cases, no tokens need to be minted or burned.

Instead, Wanchain maintains reserves of native tokens on each connected network.

For example, Alice may send USDC from Cardano to Bob on Ethereum.

The bridge locks USDC on Cardano and verifies the lock event. It then unlocks the equivalent amount of USDC from its reserves on Ethereum and sends it to Bob.

Because both sides use native tokens, the bridge only needs sufficient reserves to fulfill the transfer.

Wanchain currently connects Cardano with multiple blockchain networks. 

According to @wanchain_org statistics, the bridge has processed over $130 million in cross-chain transfer volume.

text:
@JureKaramarko @stuff_io This is no longer just a case of show up and click.
At this point, you actually need proper technique if you do not want to miss the buy. 😄
First things first: check in advance that you have enough $ADA in your #Cardano wallet.
Do not leave that for the last 15 seconds like you are cramming for a math exam.
By around 8:58 PM, you should already be connected, logged in, ready, and mentally locked in.

Second: stay calm.
Do not let the adrenaline, FOMO, or rocket launch energy take over.
Once panic kicks in, people start clicking the wrong thing, sending $ADA too fast without thinking, or making some random mistake and then later asking, What happened?
What happened is simple: Friday was faster than you. 

My recommendation is not to stare at the screen like you are under hypnosis during those final two minutes.
Try thinking about something else for a moment.
Breathe normally.
Take 5 deep breaths if needed.
Relax your fingers. Shake them out a little so they are in minting condition.
You need to enter that proper zen state… not too excited, not panicking, just calm, focused, and smooth.

When the clock hits, you are ready.
Relaxed, focused, and not making stupid mistakes.
Your ADA goes where it needs to go, the mint goes through smoothly, and afterward you act like you had everything under control the whole time. 😎

And if you are 2 or 3 minutes late…
do not lie to yourself.
You probably missed the sale, and all that is left is watching everyone else celebrate in the comments.
In that case: better luck next Friday. 
On Fridays, you are not just buying an #NFT song.
You are testing your nerves, your discipline, and the speed of your fingers. 🎵🚀

Friday on #Cardano is not for the weak. 😄

text:
I am honestly surprised people actually still use any OpenAI models for coding.

As both a researcher and developer, I use AI models in a variety of capacities.

ChatGPT has its uses, for example structured extraction from unstructured text, but it lags so far behind in coding and research relative to Claude and Gemini that I dont quite understand why people still use it for coding tasks.

No, you cant trust the graphs. Me and my team of AI researchers always laugh at the absurd charts they put out with every new model. The real test is how it performs in the trenches, not benchmarks that it likely had knowledge and training data on.

author: ADAFrog_Pool

text:
The Core Issue: CR10 (Top 10 Concentration Rate) exceeds 48% This creates tangible risks:
- Voting power controlled by a minority
- Vulnerability to governance attacks
- Power entrenchment and corruption risks
- Barriers to new DRep entry
- Exclusion of diverse voices

I am sharing my Two-layered Solution:
1️⃣ Hard Cap at 87M (1.5% limit)
✅ Structurally limits single DRep influence
✅ Increases cost of multi-DRep splitting
✅ Expands opportunities for new/small DReps

2️⃣ Annual 1-2 Delegation Resets
✅ Breaks default delegation inertia
✅ Periodically raises governance awareness
✅ Reduces passive delegation
✅ Encourages conscious re-selection (Combined with staking reward lock)
✅ Reduces delegation on inactive DReps
✅ Provides entry opportunities for new DReps
While this creates some UX friction for users, requiring delegation review 1-2 times per year can increase governance engagement.
This would lead Cardano towards a healthier ecosystem.
#CardanoGovernance #Target15

text:
"Governance Architecture and Enforcement
The governance architecture of a dOSPO serves a single objective: enable coordinated execution without allowing authority to consolidate. This requires explicit separation of powers, bounded mandates, and enforcement mechanisms that do not rely on trust or permanence.
A dOSPO operates through three policy domains, each governed by a distinct community-mandated body. These domains are intentionally general so that any sufficiently mature Web3 ecosystem can adapt them to its own governance structures."

Read more here: https://t.co/P5h0PJmcnY

text:
MCC Monthly Update - February 2026

February was a busy month for the Intersect Membership & Community Committee (MCC). Some of the main topics worked on included continued collaboration on the hubs initiative, updates regarding outreach, refinement of objectives for social media and education efforts, and work toward the finalization of the funding proposal for the new fiscal year.

Committee members and volunteers continued collaborating on the plan for the revitalization of hubs. Discussion took place throughout the month to ensure a robust plan is formulated for optimal success of the hub effort.

Other MCC objectives moved forward during the month of February included the completion and submission of materials for guidance in creating a standardized voice and structure for messaging representing Intersect across social media platforms.

Additionally, a new member welcome and orientation page was created and submitted for final approval.

Social media and education initiatives formed part of discussion at committee meetings throughout the month. Feedback from committee members helped to further fine-tune messaging and ensure social media posts align with the outreach and education mission of the MCC and of Intersect as a whole.

MCC Open Office X Spaces were held each Monday of the month. This allowed opportunities for the broader community to ask questions directly to MCC members and volunteers, gain clarity on concerns, and discuss topics on the MCC agenda.

Another significant subject of discussion and collaboration that continued in the month of February was work on finalization of the funding proposal.
Feedback was requested and discussion occurred regarding refining the final proposal, optimizing the suggested areas for funding to ensure the highest possible effectiveness in the projected use of funds for various areas under the purview of of the MCC,  and ensuring the proposed allocation of funds reflects the needs of the MCC throughout the upcoming fiscal year.

Check out the Intersect Discord channel for more updates, meeting times, and ways to get involved!

author: ItsBitcoinWorld
cash_callinAI

text:
@CoinMarketCap Cardano adoption at retail stores is one of those steps that looks small but matters over time.

When crypto like ADA can be spent in real places like grocery stores, it moves the conversation from speculation to utility. People stop asking what the price will do and start asking what the network can actually be used for.

Switzerland tends to move first with financial experimentation, so integrations like this are often a testing ground for what might expand elsewhere later.

Real adoption usually starts quietly like this before it becomes obvious.
